title: Link layer sender identification requires brute-force over all peers
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:456-505

**Risk:** The `_find_sender()` method must try every known peer's public key to verify who sent a frame (lines 500-504). The frame format does not include sender identification.

**Trigger:** Large mesh with many peers (>100). Each received frame requires O(n) signature verifications.

**Bad behavior:** Quadratic CPU cost as mesh grows. On constrained devices, this becomes a denial-of-service vector: flood the receiver with frames from unknown senders, each requiring verification against all known peers.

**Maintainer burden:** Users will report performance issues with larger meshes. The TODO on line 495 acknowledges this limitation.

**Fix:** Add sender IID to the frame format (as noted in comments), or use a bloom filter/hint to narrow down candidates.
